info.name: Mist API - Assets Mapping Controller
info.description:
Postman collection for the Mist API endpoints used by the assets-mapping-controller project.

Includes endpoints for:
- Credential validation
- Organizations
- Sites
- Site Devices
- Organization Networks
- Site Wireless Clients
- Site Wired Clients
- Site Networks (Derived)
- Site Maps

**Authentication:**
Set the `apiKey` collection variable to your Mist API token.
The `Authorization` header will be sent as `Token <apiKey>`.

**Paging:**
Use `limit` (default 1000) and `page` query parameters for paginated endpoints.

variable.description: Mist API base URL. Other regions: https://api.gc1.mist.com, https://api.eu.mist.com, https://api.ac5.mist.com
Mist API token. Used in Authorization header as: Token <apiKey>
Site ID (UUID). Obtained from listOrgSites response

request.description:
Validates API credentials by retrieving the current user's profile. Used by the spec validation step.

Returns the authenticated user's email and org privileges (including org_id values needed for other endpoints).

The `privileges[].org_id` values in the response are used to populate the `org_id` path parameter for organization-scoped requests.

item.name: Organizations
item.description: Organization-level endpoints. An organization is a top-level container for all Mist resources.

request.description:
Get Organization information.

OperationId: `getOrg`

Returns details of the organization identified by `org_id`. The `org_id` can be discovered from `GET /api/v1/self` under `privileges[].org_id`.

item.name: Sites
item.description: Sites endpoints. A site is a physical location where Mist devices are deployed.

query.description: Maximum number of records to return (default: 1000)
Page number for pagination (starts at 1)

request.description:
Get list of all sites for an organization.

OperationId: `listOrgSites`

Pagination: Use `limit` and `page` query params. Total count is returned in the `X-Page-Total` response header.

The `id` field from each site is used as `site_id` for site-scoped endpoints.

item.name: Site Devices
item.description: Site device stats. A device is physical network hardware (AP, switch, gateway) managed through Mist.

query.description: Device type filter. Values: all, ap, switch, gateway, mxedge, mxagent (spec uses 'all')
Device status filter. Values: all, connected, disconnected
Maximum number of records to return (default: 1000)
Page number for pagination (starts at 1)

request.description:
Get list of all device stats for a site.

OperationId: `listSiteDevicesStats`

The spec configures `type=all` to retrieve all device types. The `num_clients` field is returned as a JSON type.

Pagination: Use `limit` and `page` query params. Total count is returned in the `X-Page-Total` response header.

item.name: Organization Networks
item.description: Organization network endpoints. Networks are logical groupings of devices and resources.

query.description: Maximum number of records to return (default: 1000)
Page number for pagination (starts at 1)

request.description:
Get list of org-level networks.

OperationId: `listOrgNetworks`

Networks can be used to segment and organize devices based on function, location, or other criteria.

Pagination: Use `limit` and `page` query params. Total count is returned in the `X-Page-Total` response header.

item.name: Site Wireless Clients
item.description: Wireless client stats per site. Wireless clients connect via Wi-Fi to access points.

query.description: Set to false for wireless clients (default: false)
Maximum number of records to return (default: 1000)
Start time as epoch (seconds). Mutually exclusive with duration.
End time as epoch (seconds). Mutually exclusive with duration.
Duration (e.g. '1d', '1h', '30m'). Default: '1d'.

request.description:
Get list of all wireless client stats for a site.

OperationId: `listSiteWirelessClientsStats`

The response is returned under the `results` array (allOf selector in spec). The `mac` field is the primary key.

Pagination: Use `limit` query param. Total count is returned in the `X-Page-Total` response header.

item.name: Site Wired Clients
item.description: Wired client search per site. Wired clients connect via Ethernet cables.

query.description: Maximum number of records to return (default: 1000)
Filter by device MAC address (e.g. 0123456789ab)
Filter by client MAC address (e.g. 0123456789ab)
Filter by client IP address (e.g. 10.3.5.12)
Filter by port ID (e.g. ge-1/1/1)
Filter by learn source: lldp or mac
Filter by VLAN (e.g. 10)
Filter by manufacturer (e.g. Juniper-Mist)
Search by hostname or MAC (e.g. client-hostname)
Filter by NAC rule ID (e.g. 8bfc2490-d726-3587-038d-cb2e71bd2330)
Filter by DHCP hostname

request.description:
Search for wired clients at a site.

OperationId: `searchSiteWiredClients`

The response is returned under the `results` array (allOf selector in spec). The `mac` field is the primary key.

Pagination: Uses `limit` query param and link-based pagination. Next page URL is found in the `next` field of the response body.

item.name: Site Networks (Derived)
item.description: Derived networks for a site. Resolves site-level variables applied to network configurations.

query.description: Whether to resolve site variables (spec sets this to true)

request.description:
Get the list of derived networks for a site.

OperationId: `listSiteNetworksDerived`

Derived networks apply site-level variables to the organization network definitions. The spec configures `resolve=true`.

The `id` field is the primary key.

item.name: Site Maps
item.description: Site map endpoints. Maps represent floor plans and layouts of a site.

query.description: Maximum number of records to return (default: 1000)
Page number for pagination (starts at 1)

request.description:
Get list of maps for a site.

OperationId: `listSiteMaps`

Maps represent floor plans or layouts of a site, including geographical coordinates, dimensions, and visual overlays.

The `id` field is the primary key. The spec overrides field types: `ppm`, `origin_x`, and `origin_y` are cast to float64.

Pagination: Use `limit` and `page` query params. Total count is returned in the `X-Page-Total` response header.
